name: TAO 4h Session Impulse Hold Expansion v1
edge_mechanism: a specific session impulse establishes directional control, the first structural pullback holds, and expansion continues into the next leg
thesis: Cycle 78 explicitly asks for opening-range or session-window continuation when a specific session structurally dominates follow-through. TAO 4h is the natural home lane because it is the current primary lane and the only allowed asset with meaningful trend-up contamination. The edge is not raw breakout chasing. It is entering only when the impulse survives the first structural pullback and holds control.
invalidation_condition: If TAO 4h session impulses do not survive the first pullback or remain too sparse to clear screen density, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A session impulse takes control, the first retrace holds above fast structure, and continuation expands from that held control zone.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Trend-capable assets often hand off directional control around session transitions. If that first pullback holds, trapped countertrend inventory can fuel the next expansion.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because it is the live trend-up primary lane and has enough move size for session-dominant continuation to matter.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Entries may still be late if the session impulse is already exhausted by the time the hold confirms.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If trade count is good but runner contribution is weak, move more size to the first partial before changing the impulse-hold grammar.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A modest number of impulse-hold entries, several first-target wins, and a smaller number of larger continuation bursts.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h clears density and produces positive first-target expectancy with at least one cross-lane preserving the same mechanism.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-12 trades, or repeated impulse-hold entries that lose session control immediately.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because the hold requirement is simple and TAO is trend-capable

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit trend-capable lane for session impulse continuation.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h shows enough impulse-hold expansions with positive first-target expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h impulses either undertrade or fail immediately after hold confirmation.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with several continuation bursts and a few larger runners.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same session-hold grammar survives in a flatter, similar major.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h preserves a smaller but tradable impulse-hold structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the concept needs TAO trend contamination.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower amplitude, lower count continuation than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same session-control logic survives a weaker cross-lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: AXS 4h retains enough impulse-hold events to show portability.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: AXS 4h is too weak or too trend-down contaminated for the idea.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count and more fragile continuation than TAO.

long: Next bar low stays >= EMA_20 - 0.10 * ATR_14
short: Next bar high stays <= EMA_20 + 0.10 * ATR_14
